description: Primary Trickbot C2 server — highest packet volume external IP. Served fake image URIs masking C2 data. Used with WinHTTP loader/1.0 UA.
notes: No SNI on port 80; all GET requests return binary payload disguised as PNG/ICO

description: Primary Cobalt Strike C2 beacon — periodic TLS ClientHello with no SNI at ~202s intervals. Longest running C2 channel in PCAP (frames 791–26477 across full 80-minute window).
notes: JA3: 6734f37431670b3ab4292b8f60f29984; No SNI; ~202s beacon cadence confirmed

description: Secondary Cobalt Strike C2 beacon — same JA3 and ~202s interval pattern as IOC-002. Active on DESKTOP-OG16DGY (10.5.26.4) post-lateral movement.
notes: JA3: 6734f37431670b3ab4292b8f60f29984; Mirrors IOC-002 behavior; second C2 channel

description: Trickbot C2 on non-standard port 447 (not 443). Large inbound data transfers observed (frames 11437 1407B; 11451–11457 multiple 1514B frames) indicating module download or config push.
notes: Non-standard port 447 used to evade port-based filtering; JA3: 6734f37431670b3ab4292b8f60f29984

description: Trickbot C2 — TLS with no SNI. Active early in infection timeline alongside 37.228.70.134. Likely Trickbot plugin download or botnet check-in.
notes: JA3: 6734f37431670b3ab4292b8f60f29984; No SNI; co-active with IOC-002

description: Early Trickbot C2 contact — first TLS ClientHello in PCAP at frame 750. Likely initial beaconing/registration. Short-lived session.
notes: JA3: 6734f37431670b3ab4292b8f60f29984; First external C2 contact in capture

description: Trickbot C2 — TLS no SNI. Low packet count suggests periodic check-in or failed connection attempt.
TLS C2 to antivirusupdaty.com — fake antivirus update domain using suspicious Android Pixel C user-agent string. Persistent late-stage traffic suggesting exfil or module loading.
notes: SNI: antivirusupdaty.com; Fake UA spoofing Android WebView; IOC-018 cross-reference

description: Cobalt Strike staging/C2 — served malicious payloads via /rob87/ and /tot108/ URIs with bot ID and host fingerprint embedded in path. UA: 'Winhttp 1/0'.
notes: Cobalt Strike malleable C2 profile pattern; URI includes hostname+hash; IOC-013 cross-reference

description: Cobalt Strike staging/C2 — redundant C2 channel mirroring IOC-009 with identical URI patterns. Both IPs likely configured as team server listeners.
notes: Duplicate C2 for redundancy; same URI schema as IOC-009; IOC-014 cross-reference

description: IP reconnaissance — TLS connection to api.ip.sb (Cloudflare-fronted). Used by malware to identify victim external IP address pre-exfiltration.
notes: SNI: api.ip.sb; JA3: 1d095e68489d3c535297cd8dffb06cb9

description: IP reconnaissance — HTTP GET to api.ipify.org /?format=text. Malware resolving victim public IP early in infection (frame 818).
notes: UA: curl/7.74.0; Host: api.ipify.org

description: IP reconnaissance — HTTP GET to ipinfo.io/ip and myexternalip.com/raw. Two distinct recon tools active at different stages of the infection.
notes: UA: curl/7.76.0 (ipinfo) and curl/7.69.1 (myexternalip); two GETs to different recon services

description: Malicious domain masquerading as antivirus update service. TLS SNI observed in 13 ClientHellos to 5.199.162.3. Fake Android WebView UA used to evade detection.
notes: Typosquatting/masquerade; SNI confirmed in TLS handshake; cross-reference IOC-008

description: Public IP lookup service queried by Trickbot for victim IP reconnaissance at early infection stage (frame 818).
notes: Legitimate service abused; DNS query at frames 809–814

description: Public IP lookup service — Cloudflare-fronted. Accessed via TLS at frame 6921.
notes: Legitimate service abused; SNI confirmed in TLS handshake

description: Public IP lookup service queried at frame 14878 — late stage recon after lateral movement to DC.
notes: Legitimate service abused; GET /raw; UA: curl/7.69.1

description: Public IP lookup service queried at frame 10962.
notes: Legitimate service abused; GET /ip; UA: curl/7.76.0

description: Trickbot C2 URI — fake PNG filename masking binary C2 command/response data to 192.236.155.230. Observed in frames 3052 5599 13332 15645.
notes: URI used by Trickbot HTTP module for task polling; WinHTTP loader/1.0 UA

description: Trickbot C2 URI — fake PNG masking C2 traffic to 192.236.155.230. Observed frames 10876 17677.

description: Trickbot C2 URI — ICO endpoint masking C2 data to 192.236.155.230. Observed frames 4776 14741.

description: Cobalt Strike malleable C2 staging URI — encodes compromised hostname (DESKTOP-OG16DGY) + campaign ID (W617601) + hash. Served by 36.95.27.243 and 103.102.220.50.
notes: Classic CS staging URI format; bot_id/hostname_campaignid_hash/pipe_id

description: Cobalt Strike malleable C2 staging URI — encodes DC hostname (VICTORYPUNK-DC) post-lateral movement. Confirms DC compromise. Served by 36.95.27.243 and 103.102.220.50.
notes: DC hostname in URI confirms successful lateral movement to domain controller

description: Shared JA3 client fingerprint across all Trickbot and Cobalt Strike TLS sessions. Present in all no-SNI connections to IOC-001 through IOC-007. Known Trickbot JA3 hash.
notes: Single JA3 across all C2 channels indicates shared TLS library; strong detection signal

description: JA3 fingerprint for TLS connection to api.ip.sb (172.67.75.172). Different from primary malware JA3 — may indicate separate tool or binary.
notes: Only 1 occurrence; different from IOC-024; possible second-stage recon tool

description: Trickbot malware user-agent string. Present in all authenticated Trickbot HTTP C2 requests to 192.236.155.230. Definitive Trickbot IoC.
notes: Frames: 5599 10876 15645 17677; matches known Trickbot WinHTTP module UA

description: Cobalt Strike beacon user-agent string in malleable C2 profile. Present in all /rob87/ and /tot108/ requests. Definitive Cobalt Strike IoC.
notes: Frames: 2910 3045 13210 14613; CS malleable C2 UA; non-standard capitalisation is detection artifact

description: Fake Android WebView user-agent used in TLS sessions to antivirusupdaty.com (5.199.162.3). Spoofs Google Pixel C tablet to evade signature-based detection.
notes: Windows host spoofing Android UA; used exclusively with antivirusupdaty.com C2

description: Non-standard TLS port 447 used by 78.186.110.14 for C2 communications. Likely used to bypass firewall rules that filter 443 but allow adjacent ports.
notes: Non-Standard Port technique; JA3: 6734f37431670b3ab4292b8f60f29984 confirms malware TLS

description: Infected workstation — primary victim host (10.5.26.4). Initial infection point. Sources all early C2 traffic including Trickbot HTTP and Cobalt Strike beacons to IOC-002.

description: Cobalt Strike C2 behavioral IOC — TLS ClientHello with no SNI at statistically regular ~202-second intervals to 37.228.70.134 and 45.229.71.211. Interval confirmed across 28 beacon cycles spanning full PCAP duration.
Multiple internal hosts (10.5.26.130/134/136/138/140) accessing SYSVOL GPO path on DC. Pattern consistent with Trickbot lateral spread via Group Policy or Cobalt Strike SMB lateral movement.
notes: Path: victorypunk.com\Policies\{31B2F340...}\gpt.ini; 5 distinct internal hosts affected
